caSecretName.description:
The name of the secret which contains key named "ca.crt". Setting this enables the
download link on portal to download the CA certificate when the certificate isn't
generated automatically

cache.description:
cache layer configurations
if this feature enabled, harbor will cache the resource
`project/project_metadata/repository/artifact/manifest` in the redis
which help to improve the performance of high concurrent pulling manifest.

expireHours.description: default keep cache for one day.

configureUserSettings.description:
Example:
- name: wait
image: busybox
command: [ 'sh', '-c', "sleep 20" ]
User settings configuration json string

quotaUpdateProvider.description:
The provider for updating project quota(usage), there are 2 options, redis or db.
By default it is implemented by db but you can configure it to redis which
can improve the performance of high concurrent pushing to the same project,
and reduce the database connections spike and occupies.
Using redis will bring up some delay for quota usage updation for display, so only
suggest switch provider to redis if you were ran into the db connections spike around
the scenario of high concurrent pushing to same project, no improvment for other scenes.
Or redis

secret.description:
Secret is used when core server communicates with other components.
If a secret key is not specified, Helm will generate one. Alternatively set existingSecret to use an existing secret
Must be a string of 16 chars.

serviceAccountName.description: set the service account to be used, default if left empty

tokenCert.description: If tokenKey is set, the value of tokenCert must be set as a PEM-encoded certificate signed by tokenKey, and supplied as a multiline string, indented one more than tokenCert on the following line.

tokenKey.description:
If not specifying a preexisting secret, a secret can be created from tokenKey and tokenCert and used instead.
If none of secretName, tokenKey, and tokenCert are specified, an ephemeral key and certificate will be autogenerated.
tokenKey and tokenCert must BOTH be set or BOTH unset.
The tokenKey value is formatted as a multiline string containing a PEM-encoded RSA key, indented one more than tokenKey on the following line.

xsrfKey.description:
The XSRF key. Will be generated automatically if it isn't specified
While you specified, Please make sure it is 32 characters, otherwise would have validation issue at the harbor-core runtime
https://github.com/goharbor/harbor/pull/21154

existingSecret.description: if using existing secret, the key must be "password"

maxIdleConns.description:
The maximum number of connections in the idle connection pool per pod (core+exporter).
If it <=0, no idle connections are retained.

maxOpenConns.description:
The maximum number of open connections to the database per pod (core+exporter).
If it <= 0, then there is no limit on the number of open connections.
Note: the default number of connections is 1024 for harbor's postgres.

type.description:
if external database is used, set "type" to "external"
and fill the connection information in "external" section

cacheDuration.description:
- maxSkew: 1
topologyKey: topology.kubernetes.io/zone
nodeTaintsPolicy: Honor
whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule

controller.description:
set to the type of ingress controller if it has specific requirements.
leave as `default` for most ingress controllers.
set to `gce` if using the GCE ingress controller
set to `ncp` if using the NCP (NSX-T Container Plugin) ingress controller
set to `alb` if using the ALB ingress controller
set to `f5-bigip` if using the F5 BIG-IP ingress controller

kubeVersionOverride.description: Allow .Capabilities.KubeVersion.Version to be overridden while creating ingress

certSource.description:
The source of the tls certificate. Set as "auto", "secret"
or "none" and fill the information in the corresponding section
1) auto: generate the tls certificate automatically
2) secret: read the tls certificate from the specified secret.
The tls certificate can be generated manually or by cert manager
3) none: configure no tls certificate for the ingress. If the default
tls certificate is configured in the ingress controller, choose this option

type.description:
Set how to expose the service. Set the type as "ingress", "clusterIP", "nodePort", "loadBalancer" or "route"
and fill the information in the corresponding section

externalURL.description:
The external URL for Harbor core service. It is used to
1) populate the docker/helm commands showed on portal
2) populate the token service URL returned to docker client
Format: protocol://domain[:port]. Usually:
1) if "expose.type" is "ingress", the "domain" should be
the value of "expose.ingress.hosts.core"
2) if "expose.type" is "clusterIP", the "domain" should be
the value of "expose.clusterIP.name"
3) if "expose.type" is "nodePort", the "domain" should be
the IP address of k8s node
If Harbor is deployed behind the proxy, set it as the URL of proxy

internalTLS.description:
The internal TLS used for harbor components secure communicating. In order to enable https
in each component tls cert files need to provided in advance.

certSource.description:
There are three ways to provide tls
1) "auto" will generate cert automatically
2) "manual" need provide cert file manually in following value
3) "secret" internal certificates from secret

internalTLS.description:
The internal TLS used for harbor components secure communicating. In order to enable https
in each component tls cert files need to provided in advance.

ipv4.description: ipv4Enabled set to true if ipv4 is enabled in cluster, currently it affected the nginx related component

ipv6.description: ipv6Enabled set to true if ipv6 is enabled in cluster, currently it affected the nginx related component

registryHttpClientTimeout.description:
By default, the timeout is set to 30 minutes. This field only needs be re-configured if a longer request timeout is required.
the value assigned to timeout should be a number without min, e.g. registryHttpClientTimeout: 60 configures the timeout to 60 minutes

secret.description:
Secret is used when job service communicates with other components.
If a secret key is not specified, Helm will generate one.
Must be a string of 16 chars.

logLevel.description: debug, info, warning, error or fatal

serviceMonitor.description:
Create prometheus serviceMonitor to scrape harbor metrics.
This requires the monitoring.coreos.com/v1 CRD. Please see
https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/blob/main/Documentation/user-guides/getting-started.md

persistence.description:
The persistence is enabled by default and a default StorageClass
is needed in the k8s cluster to provision volumes dynamically.
Specify another StorageClass in the "storageClass" or set "existingClaim"
if you already have existing persistent volumes to use
For storing images and charts, you can also use "azure", "gcs", "s3",
"swift" or "oss". Set it in the "imageChartStorage" section

imageChartStorage.description:
Define which storage backend is used for registry to store
images and charts. Refer to
https://github.com/distribution/distribution/blob/release/2.8/docs/configuration.md#storage
for the detail.

oss.description:
region: fr
tenant: tenantname
tenantid: tenantid
domain: domainname
domainid: domainid
trustid: trustid
insecureskipverify: false
chunksize: 5M
prefix:
secretkey: secretkey
accesskey: accesskey
authversion: 3
endpointtype: public
tempurlcontainerkey: false
tempurlmethods:

swift.description:
accesskey: awsaccesskey
secretkey: awssecretkey
regionendpoint: http://myobjects.local
encrypt: false
keyid: mykeyid
secure: true
skipverify: false
v4auth: true
chunksize: "5242880"
rootdirectory: /s3/object/name/prefix
storageclass: STANDARD
multipartcopychunksize: "33554432"
multipartcopymaxconcurrency: 100
multipartcopythresholdsize: "33554432"

is needed in the k8s cluster to provision volumes dynamically.
Specify another StorageClass in the "storageClass" or set "existingClaim"
if you already have existing persistent volumes to use
For storing images and charts, you can also use "azure", "gcs", "s3",
"swift" or "oss". Set it in the "imageChartStorage" section

be ignored

resourcePolicy.description:
Setting it to "keep" to avoid removing PVCs during a helm delete
operation. Leaving it empty will delete PVCs after the chart deleted
(this does not apply for PVCs that are created for internal database
and redis components, i.e. they are never deleted automatically)

persistence.description:
The persistence is enabled by default and a default StorageClass
is needed in the k8s cluster to provision volumes dynamically.
Specify another StorageClass in the "storageClass" or set "existingClaim"
if you already have existing persistent volumes to use
For storing images and charts, you can also use "azure", "gcs", "s3",
"swift" or "oss". Set it in the "imageChartStorage" section

proxy.description:
The proxy settings for updating trivy vulnerabilities from the Internet and replicating
artifacts from/to the registries that cannot be reached directly

tlsOptions.description:
TLS configuration for redis connection
only server-authentication is supported, mTLS for redis connection is not supported
tls connection will be disable by default
Once `tlsOptions.enable` set as true, tls/ssl connection will be used for redis
Please set the `caBundleSecretName` in this configuration file which conatins redis server rootCA if it is self-signed.
The secret must contain keys named "ca.crt" which will be injected into the trust store

type.description:
if external Redis is used, set "type" to "external"
and fill the connection information in "external" section

htpasswdString.description:
Login and password in htpasswd string format. Excludes `registry.credentials.username`  and `registry.credentials.password`. May come in handy when integrating with tools like argocd or flux. This allows the same line to be generated each time the template is rendered, instead of the `htpasswd` function from helm, which generates different lines each time because of the salt.
htpasswdString: "harbor_registry_user:$2a$05$IPnI.TSgP8ek1/ESjkULVemA.A3nSXPdN80ljsmcun7FtE3ViSYUq" # example string, bcrypt is the only accepted hashing algorithm in htpasswd here.

Must be a string of 16 chars.

sample_rate.description: set sample_rate to 1 if you wanna sampling 100% of trace data; set 0.5 if you wanna sampling 50% of trace data, and so forth

gitHubToken.description:
gitHubToken the GitHub access token to download Trivy DB
Trivy DB contains vulnerability information from NVD, Red Hat, and many other upstream vulnerability databases.
It is downloaded by Trivy from the GitHub release page https://github.com/aquasecurity/trivy-db/releases and cached
in the local file system (`/home/scanner/.cache/trivy/db/trivy.db`). In addition, the database contains the update
timestamp so Trivy can detect whether it should download a newer version from the Internet or use the cached one.
Currently, the database is updated every 12 hours and published as a new release to GitHub.
Anonymous downloads from GitHub are subject to the limit of 60 requests per hour. Normally such rate limit is enough
for production operations. If, for any reason, it's not enough, you could increase the rate limit to 5000
requests per hour by specifying the GitHub access token. For more details on GitHub rate limiting please consult
https://developer.github.com/v3/#rate-limiting
You can create a GitHub token by following the instructions in
https://help.github.com/en/github/authenticating-to-github/creating-a-personal-access-token-for-the-command-line

repository.description: repository the repository for Trivy adapter image

updateStrategy.description:
The update strategy for deployments with persistent volumes(jobservice, registry): "RollingUpdate" or "Recreate"
Set it as "Recreate" when "RWM" for volumes isn't supported

caSecretName.description:
The name of the secret which contains key named "ca.crt". Setting this enables the
download link on portal to download the CA certificate when the certificate isn't
generated automatically

cache.description:
cache layer configurations
if this feature enabled, harbor will cache the resource
`project/project_metadata/repository/artifact/manifest` in the redis
which help to improve the performance of high concurrent pulling manifest.

expireHours.description: default keep cache for one day.

containerSecurityContext.description:
set Container Security Context to comply with PSP restricted policy if necessary
each of the conatiner will apply the same security context
containerSecurityContext:{} is initially an empty yaml that you could edit it on demand, we just filled with a common template for convenience

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Spread Pods across failure-domains like regions, availability zones or nodes

artifactPullAsyncFlushDuration.description:
The time duration for async update artifact pull_time and repository
pull_count, the unit is second. Will be 10 seconds if it isn't set.
eg. artifactPullAsyncFlushDuration: 10

configureUserSettings.description:
Example:
- name: wait
image: busybox
command: [ 'sh', '-c', "sleep 20" ]
User settings configuration json string

existingSecret.description:
Fill in the name of a kubernetes secret if you want to use your own
If using existingSecret, the key must be secret

existingXsrfSecret.description: If using existingSecret, the key is defined by core.existingXsrfSecretKey

initContainers.description: containers to be run before the controller's container starts.

podAnnotations.description:
- maxSkew: 1
topologyKey: topology.kubernetes.io/zone
nodeTaintsPolicy: Honor
whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule
Additional deployment annotations

quotaUpdateProvider.description:
The provider for updating project quota(usage), there are 2 options, redis or db.
By default it is implemented by db but you can configure it to redis which
can improve the performance of high concurrent pushing to the same project,
and reduce the database connections spike and occupies.
Using redis will bring up some delay for quota usage updation for display, so only
suggest switch provider to redis if you were ran into the db connections spike around
the scenario of high concurrent pushing to same project, no improvment for other scenes.
Or redis

secret.description:
Secret is used when core server communicates with other components.
If a secret key is not specified, Helm will generate one. Alternatively set existingSecret to use an existing secret
Must be a string of 16 chars.

secretName.description:
Fill the name of a kubernetes secret if you want to use your own
TLS certificate and private key for token encryption/decryption.
The secret must contain keys named:
"tls.key" - the private key
"tls.crt" - the certificate

serviceAccountName.description: set the service account to be used, default if left empty

tokenCert.description: If tokenKey is set, the value of tokenCert must be set as a PEM-encoded certificate signed by tokenKey, and supplied as a multiline string, indented one more than tokenCert on the following line.

tokenKey.description:
If not specifying a preexisting secret, a secret can be created from tokenKey and tokenCert and used instead.
If none of secretName, tokenKey, and tokenCert are specified, an ephemeral key and certificate will be autogenerated.
tokenKey and tokenCert must BOTH be set or BOTH unset.
The tokenKey value is formatted as a multiline string containing a PEM-encoded RSA key, indented one more than tokenKey on the following line.

xsrfKey.description:
The XSRF key. Will be generated automatically if it isn't specified
While you specified, Please make sure it is 32 characters, otherwise would have validation issue at the harbor-core runtime
https://github.com/goharbor/harbor/pull/21154

serviceAccountName.description: set the service account to be used, default if left empty

shmSizeLimit.description:
The size limit for Shared memory, pgSQL use it for shared_buffer
More details see:
https://github.com/goharbor/harbor/issues/15034

existingSecret.description: if using existing secret, the key must be "password"

sslmode.description:
"disable" - No SSL
"require" - Always SSL (skip verification)
"verify-ca" - Always SSL (verify that the certificate presented by the
server was signed by a trusted CA)
"verify-full" - Always SSL (verify that the certification presented by the
server was signed by a trusted CA and the server host name matches the one
in the certificate)

password.description:
Example:
- name: wait
image: busybox
command: [ 'sh', '-c', "sleep 20" ]
The initial superuser password for internal database

shmSizeLimit.description:
The size limit for Shared memory, pgSQL use it for shared_buffer
More details see:
https://github.com/goharbor/harbor/issues/15034

maxIdleConns.description:
The maximum number of connections in the idle connection pool per pod (core+exporter).
If it <=0, no idle connections are retained.

maxOpenConns.description:
The maximum number of open connections to the database per pod (core+exporter).
If it <= 0, then there is no limit on the number of open connections.
Note: the default number of connections is 1024 for harbor's postgres.

type.description:
if external database is used, set "type" to "external"
and fill the connection information in "external" section

enableMigrateHelmHook.description: Run the migration job via helm hook

existingSecretAdminPassword.description:
The initial password of Harbor admin. Change it from portal after launching Harbor
or give an existing secret for it
key in secret is given via (default to HARBOR_ADMIN_PASSWORD)

existingSecretSecretKey.description: If using existingSecretSecretKey, the key must be secretKey

nodeTaintsPolicy: Honor
whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule

IP.description: Set the IP if the LoadBalancer supports assigning IP

nodePort.description: The node port Harbor listens on when serving HTTP
The node port Harbor listens on when serving HTTPS

secretName.description:
The name of secret which contains keys named:
"tls.crt" - the certificate
"tls.key" - the private key

staticClusterIP.description: The ip address of the ClusterIP service (leave empty for acquiring dynamic ip)

controller.description:
set to the type of ingress controller if it has specific requirements.
leave as `default` for most ingress controllers.
set to `gce` if using the GCE ingress controller
set to `ncp` if using the NCP (NSX-T Container Plugin) ingress controller
set to `alb` if using the ALB ingress controller
set to `f5-bigip` if using the F5 BIG-IP ingress controller

kubeVersionOverride.description: Allow .Capabilities.KubeVersion.Version to be overridden while creating ingress

IP.description: Set the IP if the LoadBalancer supports assigning IP

nodePort.description: The node port Harbor listens on when serving HTTP
The node port Harbor listens on when serving HTTPS

hosts.description:
- name: envoy-internal
namespace: networking
sectionName: https
group: gateway.networking.k8s.io
kind: Gateway
The hostnames for the HTTPRoute

commonName.description:
The common name used to generate the certificate, it's necessary
when the type isn't "ingress"

certSource.description:
The source of the tls certificate. Set as "auto", "secret"
or "none" and fill the information in the corresponding section
1) auto: generate the tls certificate automatically
2) secret: read the tls certificate from the specified secret.
The tls certificate can be generated manually or by cert manager
3) none: configure no tls certificate for the ingress. If the default
tls certificate is configured in the ingress controller, choose this option

enabled.description:
Enable TLS or not.
Delete the "ssl-redirect" annotations in "expose.ingress.annotations" when TLS is disabled and "expose.type" is "ingress"
Note: if the "expose.type" is "ingress" and TLS is disabled,
the port must be included in the command when pulling/pushing images.
Refer to https://github.com/goharbor/harbor/issues/5291 for details.

secretName.description:
The name of secret which contains keys named:
"tls.crt" - the certificate
"tls.key" - the private key

type.description:
Set how to expose the service. Set the type as "ingress", "clusterIP", "nodePort", "loadBalancer" or "route"
and fill the information in the corresponding section

externalURL.description:
The external URL for Harbor core service. It is used to
1) populate the docker/helm commands showed on portal
2) populate the token service URL returned to docker client
Format: protocol://domain[:port]. Usually:
1) if "expose.type" is "ingress", the "domain" should be
the value of "expose.ingress.hosts.core"
2) if "expose.type" is "clusterIP", the "domain" should be
the value of "expose.clusterIP.name"
3) if "expose.type" is "nodePort", the "domain" should be
the IP address of k8s node
If Harbor is deployed behind the proxy, set it as the URL of proxy

imagePullSecrets.description:
Use this set to assign a list of default pullSecrets
- name: docker-registry-secret
- name: internal-registry-secret

internalTLS.description:
The internal TLS used for harbor components secure communicating. In order to enable https
in each component tls cert files need to provided in advance.

certSource.description:
There are three ways to provide tls
1) "auto" will generate cert automatically
2) "manual" need provide cert file manually in following value
3) "secret" internal certificates from secret

max_update_hours.description: the max time to wait for a task to finish, if unfinished after max_update_hours, the task will be mark as error, but the task will continue to run, default value is 24

By default, the timeout is set to 30 minutes. This field only needs be re-configured if a longer request timeout is required.
the value assigned to timeout should be a number without min, e.g. registryHttpClientTimeout: 60 configures the timeout to 60 minutes

and specify the "subPath" if the PVC is shared with other components

secure: true
skipverify: false
v4auth: true
chunksize: "5242880"
rootdirectory: /s3/object/name/prefix
storageclass: STANDARD
multipartcopychunksize: "33554432"
multipartcopymaxconcurrency: 100
multipartcopythresholdsize: "33554432"

imageChartStorage.description:
Define which storage backend is used for registry to store
images and charts. Refer to
https://github.com/distribution/distribution/blob/release/2.8/docs/configuration.md#storage
for the detail.

persistence.description:
The persistence is enabled by default and a default StorageClass
is needed in the k8s cluster to provision volumes dynamically.
Specify another StorageClass in the "storageClass" or set "existingClaim"
if you already have existing persistent volumes to use
For storing images and charts, you can also use "azure", "gcs", "s3",
"swift" or "oss". Set it in the "imageChartStorage" section

imageChartStorage.description:
Define which storage backend is used for registry to store
images and charts. Refer to
https://github.com/distribution/distribution/blob/release/2.8/docs/configuration.md#storage
for the detail.

existingSecret.description:
realm: core.windows.net
To use existing secret, the key must be AZURE_STORAGE_ACCESS_KEY

disableredirect.description:
Specify whether to disable `redirect` for images and chart storage, for
backends which not supported it (such as using minio for `s3` storage type), please disable
it. To disable redirects, simply set `disableredirect` to `true` instead.
Refer to
https://github.com/distribution/distribution/blob/release/2.8/docs/configuration.md#redirect
for the detail.
Specify the "caBundleSecretName" if the storage service uses a self-signed certificate.
The secret must contain keys named "ca.crt" which will be injected into the trust store
of registry's containers.
caBundleSecretName:

encodedkey.description: The base64 encoded json file which contains the key

existingSecret.description:
rootdirectory: /gcs/object/name/prefix
chunksize: "5242880"
To use existing secret, the key must be GCS_KEY_DATA

oss.description:
region: fr
tenant: tenantname
tenantid: tenantid
domain: domainname
domainid: domainid
trustid: trustid
insecureskipverify: false
chunksize: 5M
prefix:
secretkey: secretkey
accesskey: accesskey
authversion: 3
endpointtype: public
tempurlcontainerkey: false
tempurlmethods:

existingSecret.description:
key in existingSecret must be REGISTRY_STORAGE_OSS_ACCESSKEYSECRET
endpoint: endpoint
internal: false
encrypt: false
secure: true
chunksize: 10M
rootdirectory: rootdirectory

region.description:
Set an existing secret for S3 accesskey and secretkey
keys in the secret should be REGISTRY_STORAGE_S3_ACCESSKEY and REGISTRY_STORAGE_S3_SECRETKEY for registry
existingSecret: ""

swift.description:
accesskey: awsaccesskey
secretkey: awssecretkey
regionendpoint: http://myobjects.local
encrypt: false
keyid: mykeyid
secure: true
skipverify: false
v4auth: true
chunksize: "5242880"
rootdirectory: /s3/object/name/prefix
storageclass: STANDARD
multipartcopychunksize: "33554432"
multipartcopymaxconcurrency: 100
multipartcopythresholdsize: "33554432"

existingSecret.description: keys in existing secret must be REGISTRY_STORAGE_SWIFT_PASSWORD, REGISTRY_STORAGE_SWIFT_SECRETKEY, REGISTRY_STORAGE_SWIFT_ACCESSKEY

type.description:
Specify the type of storage: "filesystem", "azure", "gcs", "s3", "swift",
"oss" and fill the information needed in the corresponding section. The type
must be "filesystem" if you want to use persistent volumes for registry

database.description:
If external database is used, the following settings for database will
be ignored

redis.description:
If external Redis is used, the following settings for Redis will
be ignored

existingClaim.description:
Use the existing PVC which must be created manually before bound,
and specify the "subPath" if the PVC is shared with other components

storageClass.description:
Specify the "storageClass" used to provision the volume. Or the default
StorageClass will be used (the default).
Set it to "-" to disable dynamic provisioning

resourcePolicy.description:
Setting it to "keep" to avoid removing PVCs during a helm delete
operation. Leaving it empty will delete PVCs after the chart deleted
(this does not apply for PVCs that are created for internal database
and redis components, i.e. they are never deleted automatically)

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Spread Pods across failure-domains like regions, availability zones or nodes

initContainers.description:
containers to be run before the controller's container starts.
Example:
- name: wait
image: busybox
command: [ 'sh', '-c', "sleep 20" ]

podAnnotations.description:
- maxSkew: 1
topologyKey: topology.kubernetes.io/zone
nodeTaintsPolicy: Honor
whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule
Additional deployment annotations

serviceAccountName.description: set the service account to be used, default if left empty

proxy.description:
The proxy settings for updating trivy vulnerabilities from the Internet and replicating
artifacts from/to the registries that cannot be reached directly

serviceAccountName.description: set the service account to be used, default if left empty

addr.description:
support redis, redis+sentinel
addr for redis: <host_redis>:<port_redis>
addr for redis+sentinel: <host_sentinel1>:<port_sentinel1>,<host_sentinel2>:<port_sentinel2>,<host_sentinel3>:<port_sentinel3>

coreDatabaseIndex.description:
The "coreDatabaseIndex" must be "0" as the library Harbor
used doesn't support configuring it
harborDatabaseIndex defaults to "0", but it can be configured to "6", this config is optional
cacheLayerDatabaseIndex defaults to "0", but it can be configured to "7", this config is optional

existingSecret.description: If using existingSecret, the key must be REDIS_PASSWORD, if ACL mode enabled, also inlcudes data of username, the keys must be REDIS_USERNAME

sentinelMasterSet.description: The name of the set of Redis instances to monitor, it must be set to support redis+sentinel

tlsOptions.description:
TLS configuration for redis connection
only server-authentication is supported, mTLS for redis connection is not supported
tls connection will be disable by default
Once `tlsOptions.enable` set as true, tls/ssl connection will be used for redis
Please set the `caBundleSecretName` in this configuration file which conatins redis server rootCA if it is self-signed.
The secret must contain keys named "ca.crt" which will be injected into the trust store

username.description:
harborDatabaseIndex: "6"
cacheLayerDatabaseIndex: "7"
username field can be an empty string, and it will be authenticated against the default user

jobserviceDatabaseIndex.description:
Example:
- name: wait
image: busybox
command: [ 'sh', '-c', "sleep 20" ]
jobserviceDatabaseIndex defaults to "1"
registryDatabaseIndex defaults to "2"
trivyAdapterIndex defaults to "5"
harborDatabaseIndex defaults to "0", but it can be configured to "6", this config is optional
cacheLayerDatabaseIndex defaults to "0", but it can be configured to "7", this config is optional

type.description:
if external Redis is used, set "type" to "external"
and fill the connection information in "external" section

existingSecretKey.description: Key within the existing secret for the registry service secret

htpasswdString.description:
Login and password in htpasswd string format. Excludes `registry.credentials.username`  and `registry.credentials.password`. May come in handy when integrating with tools like argocd or flux. This allows the same line to be generated each time the template is rendered, instead of the `htpasswd` function from helm, which generates different lines each time because of the salt.
htpasswdString: "harbor_registry_user:$2a$05$IPnI.TSgP8ek1/ESjkULVemA.A3nSXPdN80ljsmcun7FtE3ViSYUq" # example string, bcrypt is the only accepted hashing algorithm in htpasswd here.

privateKeySecret.description:
The secret key that should be present is CLOUDFRONT_KEY_DATA, which should be the encoded private key
that allows access to CloudFront

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Spread Pods across failure-domains like regions, availability zones or nodes

existingSecret.description: If using existingSecret, the key must be REGISTRY_PASSWD and REGISTRY_HTPASSWD

htpasswdString.description:
Login and password in htpasswd string format. Excludes `registry.credentials.username`  and `registry.credentials.password`. May come in handy when integrating with tools like argocd or flux. This allows the same line to be generated each time the template is rendered, instead of the `htpasswd` function from helm, which generates different lines each time because of the salt.
htpasswdString: "harbor_registry_user:$2a$05$IPnI.TSgP8ek1/ESjkULVemA.A3nSXPdN80ljsmcun7FtE3ViSYUq" # example string, bcrypt is the only accepted hashing algorithm in htpasswd here.

existingSecretKey.description: Key within the existing secret for the registry service secret

initContainers.description: containers to be run before the controller's container starts.

privateKeySecret.description:
The secret key that should be present is CLOUDFRONT_KEY_DATA, which should be the encoded private key
that allows access to CloudFront

podAnnotations.description:
- maxSkew: 1
topologyKey: topology.kubernetes.io/zone
nodeTaintsPolicy: Honor
whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule
Additional deployment annotations

relativeurls.description: If true, the registry returns relative URLs in Location headers. The client is responsible for resolving the correct URL.

secret.description:
Example:
- name: wait
image: busybox
command: [ 'sh', '-c', "sleep 20" ]
Secret is used to secure the upload state from client
and registry storage backend.
See: https://github.com/distribution/distribution/blob/release/2.8/docs/configuration.md#http
If a secret key is not specified, Helm will generate one.
Must be a string of 16 chars.

serviceAccountName.description: set the service account to be used, default if left empty

age.description: remove files in _upload directories which exist for a period of time, default is one week.

interval.description: the interval of the purge operations

secretKey.description: The secret key used for encryption. Must be a string of 16 chars.

endpoint.description:
jaeger supports two modes:
collector mode(uncomment endpoint and uncomment username, password if needed)
agent mode(uncomment agent_host and agent_port)

jaeger.description:
namespace used to differentiate different harbor services
namespace:
attributes is a key value dict contains user defined attributes used to initialize trace provider
attributes:
application: harbor

otel.description:
username:
password:
agent_host: hostname
export trace data by jaeger.thrift in compact mode
agent_port: 6831

provider.description:
trace provider: jaeger or otel
jaeger should be 1.26+

sample_rate.description: set sample_rate to 1 if you wanna sampling 100% of trace data; set 0.5 if you wanna sampling 50% of trace data, and so forth

podAnnotations.description:
- maxSkew: 1
topologyKey: topology.kubernetes.io/zone
nodeTaintsPolicy: Honor
whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule
Additional deployment annotations

replicas.description: replicas the number of Pod replicas

serviceAccountName.description: set the service account to be used, default if left empty

dbRepository.description:
The dbRepository and javaDBRepository flags can take multiple values, improving reliability when downloading databases.
Databases are downloaded in priority order until one is successful.
An attempt to download from the next repository is only made if a temporary error is received (e.g. status 429 or 5xx).
OCI repository(ies) to retrieve the trivy vulnerability database in order of priority

debugMode.description:
Example:
- name: wait
image: busybox
command: [ 'sh', '-c', "sleep 20" ]
debugMode the flag to enable Trivy debug mode with more verbose scanning log

enabled.description: enabled the flag to enable Trivy scanner

gitHubToken.description:
gitHubToken the GitHub access token to download Trivy DB
Trivy DB contains vulnerability information from NVD, Red Hat, and many other upstream vulnerability databases.
It is downloaded by Trivy from the GitHub release page https://github.com/aquasecurity/trivy-db/releases and cached
in the local file system (`/home/scanner/.cache/trivy/db/trivy.db`). In addition, the database contains the update
timestamp so Trivy can detect whether it should download a newer version from the Internet or use the cached one.
Currently, the database is updated every 12 hours and published as a new release to GitHub.
Anonymous downloads from GitHub are subject to the limit of 60 requests per hour. Normally such rate limit is enough
for production operations. If, for any reason, it's not enough, you could increase the rate limit to 5000
requests per hour by specifying the GitHub access token. For more details on GitHub rate limiting please consult
https://developer.github.com/v3/#rate-limiting
You can create a GitHub token by following the instructions in
https://help.github.com/en/github/authenticating-to-github/creating-a-personal-access-token-for-the-command-line

ignoreUnfixed.description: ignoreUnfixed the flag to display only fixed vulnerabilities

repository.description: repository the repository for Trivy adapter image

tag.description: tag the tag for Trivy adapter image

insecure.description: insecure the flag to skip verifying registry certificate

javaDBRepository.description: OCI repository(ies) to retrieve the Java trivy vulnerability database in order of priority

offlineScan.description:
The offlineScan option prevents Trivy from sending API requests to identify dependencies.
Scanning JAR files and pom.xml may require Internet access for better detection, but this option tries to avoid it.
For example, the offline mode will not try to resolve transitive dependencies in pom.xml when the dependency doesn't
exist in the local repositories. It means a number of detected vulnerabilities might be fewer in offline mode.
It would work if all the dependencies are in local.
This option doesn’t affect DB download. You need to specify skipUpdate as well as offlineScan in an air-gapped environment.

replicas.description: replicas the number of Pod replicas

securityCheck.description: Comma-separated list of what security issues to detect. Defaults to `vuln`.

severity.description: severity a comma-separated list of severities to be checked

skipJavaDBUpdate.description:
skipJavaDBUpdate If the flag is enabled you have to manually download the `trivy-java.db` file and mount it in the
`/home/scanner/.cache/trivy/java-db/trivy-java.db` path

skipUpdate.description:
skipUpdate the flag to disable Trivy DB downloads from GitHub
You might want to set the value of this flag to `true` in test or CI/CD environments to avoid GitHub rate limiting issues.
If the value is set to `true` you have to manually download the `trivy.db` file and mount it in the
`/home/scanner/.cache/trivy/db/trivy.db` path.

timeout.description: The duration to wait for scan completion

vulnType.description: vulnType a comma-separated list of vulnerability types. Possible values are `os` and `library`.

updateStrategy.description:
The update strategy for deployments with persistent volumes(jobservice, registry): "RollingUpdate" or "Recreate"
Set it as "Recreate" when "RWM" for volumes isn't supported
